message.content:
Certainly! Crafting a sincere and effective apology is about taking responsibility, showing empathy, and making amends. Here’s a draft for your apology message. I’ve made sure to keep it clear, accountable, and empathetic:

---

Subject: I’m Truly Sorry for Hurting You

Hi [Friend's Name],

I want to start by expressing my deepest apologies for [specific action or incident]. I know I hurt you, and I take full responsibility for my actions. What I said/did was completely inconsiderate and thoughtless, and I realize now how it’s affected you. I should have handled the situation differently and with more care.

Your feelings are important to me, and I understand that I failed to respect them. I can’t begin to tell you how sorry I am for making you feel this way. It was never my intention to cause you pain, and I regret my words/behavior deeply.

I value our friendship and am truly sorry for any pain or hurt I caused you. I would like to make things better and assure you that I’ve been reflecting on how to treat you with more empathy and respect moving forward.

Please know that you can talk to me about this or any other issues you may have. I’m here to listen and do better if I can.

Warmly,

[Your Name]

---

Feel free to adjust the specifics to better fit your situation and relationship with your friend. The key is to be genuine and show that you understand their feelings and want to make amends.
